summary: Joby Aviation and Archer Aviation are statistically equivalent in outperformance probability (Joby 60%, Archer 55%), with Joby's 6–12 month FAA regulatory lead offset by Archer's dual-path revenue redundancy (Abu Dhabi + eIPP + USAF) and superior capital efficiency (18–24 month runway vs. Joby 17–18 months). EHang's profitability (50%) de-risks pre-revenue peer group; Beta's established revenue provides defensibility (48%); Vertical faces critical liquidity crisis (15%); Lilium in liquidation (4%). Driver-specific probabilities: Revenue Launch (Archer 75% > Joby 72%), FAA Cert (Joby 68% > Archer 58%), Battery 400Wh/kg + Non-Dilutive Capital (EHang 92% > Archer 42% > Joby 35%). Archer's highest execution risk is 252-aircraft/year production ramp with labor/battery supply constraints.

rows.label: Joby Aviation — Probability of Outperforming Sector Base Rate (12-month)

extras.prob_outperform_rationale: FAA Stage 4 regulatory lead (late 2026 Type Cert) + Dubai revenue inflection (Q2–Q3 2026 on-track) position Joby as first-mover in US market scaling. However, single-point-of-failure risk (Uber Dubai dependency) and higher burn rate ($147M/quarter) offset advantage.
extras.funding_need_rationale: Dubai delay beyond Q4 2026 triggers forced equity raise to bridge runway. Likelihood of delay in 12-month window: 25–30%, implying capital raise probability ~55%.
extras.driver_1_rationale: Dubai Q2–Q3 2026 on-track (vertiport 60% complete, Uber partnership confirmed). Single path means full miss if delayed >3 months.
extras.driver_2_rationale: Stage 4 (TIA) is furthest advanced. Late 2026 target achievable, but TIA phase prone to 3–6 month surprises per regulatory precedent.
extras.driver_3_rationale: 400 Wh/kg is sector-wide constraint, not Joby-specific. Capital: likely needs additional raise by Q4 2026; equity dilution probable (non-dilutive probability <40%).
extras.key_inflection: Dubai revenue recognition Q2–Q3 2026; Type Cert late 2026 de-risks 2027 US scaling to 4/month

rows.label: Archer Aviation — Probability of Outperforming Sector Base Rate (12-month)

extras.prob_outperform_rationale: Dual-path revenue (Abu Dhabi Q3 + eIPP H2 + USAF Q4) provides 40% risk reduction vs. Joby's single Dubai dependency. Better capital efficiency (27–40% lower quarterly burn) and non-dilutive Stellantis $400M offset FAA timing lag (6–12 months behind Joby). However, aggressive 252-aircraft ramp carries HIGH execution risk (labor, batteries).
extras.funding_need_rationale: Burn rate + capex for production ramp suggests $300–500M additional raise needed H2 2026–Q1 2027. Probability forced raise within 12 months: ~65% (dilutive unless Stellantis converts to debt or macro rates fall).
extras.driver_1_rationale: Three independent paths: Abu Dhabi (RTC approved, on-track), eIPP (pilot program, de-risked), USAF (contract already signed). If Abu Dhabi slips 3 months, eIPP + USAF provide fallback. Combined success probability higher than Joby's single Dubai.
extras.driver_2_rationale: 100% MOC Approved with Q4 2026–Q1 2027 target. 6–12 month lag vs. Joby puts certification in late 12-month window; delays compress 2027 market entry to Q2, conceding 2-quarter first-mover advantage.
extras.driver_3_rationale: Stellantis $400M non-dilutive secured. Battery 400 Wh/kg HIGH sensitivity per A2.3; supply chain tight. Additional $300–500M raise likely needed by H2 2026 (dilutive probability ~60% unless debt/Stellantis conversion).
extras.key_inflection: Abu Dhabi + eIPP revenue confirmation H2 2026; Type Cert Q4 2026–Q1 2027 enables 2027 ramp. Production execution risk: 252-aircraft target unprecedented (5× Joby); labor shortage + battery supply may reduce by 30–50%, compressing upside 40%.

rows.label: EHang Holdings — Probability of Outperforming Sector Base Rate (12-month)

extras.prob_outperform_rationale: Only profitable peer with $72.9M FY2025 revenue and positive EBITDA. Pilotless autonomous design (EH216-S) eliminates pilot wage cost (15–20% OPEX advantage). Commercial operations already underway in China (March 2026); Thailand 2026 planned. Profitability de-risks valuation vs. pre-revenue peers; outperforms on execution likelihood but capped by Asia-only TAM.
extras.funding_need_rationale: Self-funded profitable operations; near-zero debt. No capital raise required within 12 months. Probability <15%.
extras.driver_1_rationale: Operations already deployed in China (March 2026); Thailand 2026 planned. Lowest execution risk among peers. Revenue inflection already proven vs. Joby/Archer pre-revenue paths.
extras.driver_2_rationale: Pilotless autonomous design operates under CAAC (China) regulatory framework, not FAA. Non-comparable to Joby/Archer US certification timelines.
extras.driver_3_rationale: Profitable operations self-fund capex; near-zero debt; no external capital raise needed within 12 months. Pilotless design may have different battery requirements vs. crewed eVTOL.
extras.key_inflection: China + Thailand commercial scaling. Asia-only TAM limits upside relative to global air taxi (Joby/Archer). Forward EV/Revenue 8–9× (10× discount to Joby) reflects profitability and geographic constraint. Most defensible peer but least asymmetric upside.

rows.label: Beta Technologies — Probability of Outperforming Sector Base Rate (12-month)

extras.prob_outperform_rationale: Only second peer (after EHang) generating current revenue: $35.6M TTM. $4B+ backlog with established cargo/emergency customers (UPS, Air New Zealand, United Therapeutics) de-risks demand vs. passenger air taxi. Cargo OEM model caps growth vs. eVTOL passenger market but provides stability. Strong cash ($1.7B) avoids near-term capital raise. Outperforms on stability; underperforms on growth upside.
extras.funding_need_rationale: Cash position $1.7B with $500M capex planned 2026 provides cushion. Equity raise not forced, but possible if backlog slows. Probability: ~35%.
extras.driver_1_rationale: $35.6M TTM revenue already generating; $4B+ backlog. No new operational launch required within 12 months. Lowest execution risk (baseline business model).
extras.driver_2_rationale: Cargo OEM business model; FAA passenger certification not on critical path. Cert timeline years away but not blocking revenue scaling.
extras.driver_3_rationale: Strong cash position; $1.7B balance sheet supports $500M capex. Battery requirements differ from passenger eVTOL (cargo, emergency services lower power envelope). Capital raise not needed within 12 months.
extras.key_inflection: Backlog conversion into revenue. Valuation collapsed from $7.44B IPO to $3.38B (54% drawdown, March 2026); forward EV/Revenue 95× indicates market pricing in cert delay and TAM constraints. Cargo TAM capped ($1–2B annually vs. air taxi $10–50B); growth ceiling is structural limitation relative to Joby/Archer.

rows.label: Vertical Aerospace — Probability of Outperforming Sector Base Rate (12-month)

extras.prob_outperform_rationale: CRITICAL LIQUIDITY CRISIS dominates outlook. Cash position GBP 96M (~$121M) vs. 12-month burn GBP 180–200M (~$227–252M) creates 6–12 month runway. Must raise $200M+ by Q2 2026 or face insolvency. FAA VX4 certification not expected until end-2028 (20+ months behind Joby); no revenue bridge within 12-month horizon. Only outperforms (15% probability) if recapitalization succeeds at favorable terms—low likelihood.
extras.funding_need_rationale: Survival depends on capital raise by Q2 2026. Facility available ($850M limit) but requires equity conversion at distressed terms. Probability of forced raise: ~92%. Dilution >30% probable.
extras.driver_1_rationale: No revenue event expected within 12 months. Pre-revenue phase with unclear launch timeline. easyJet interest exists but no firm orders.
extras.driver_2_rationale: VX4 Type Certification targeted end-2028; 20+ months behind Joby. Early-stage FAA engagement (vs. Joby Stage 4). Significant slippage risk on already-delayed timeline.
extras.driver_3_rationale: Must raise $200M+ capital by Q2 2026 to continue operations. Any capital raise will be highly dilutive (>30% ownership dilution). Probability of non-dilutive raise: <20%. Mudrick Capital committed $50M equity + $130M debt-to-equity, but terms likely unfavorable to legacy holders.
extras.key_inflection: Q2 2026 capital raise success. Stock price collapsed $7.60 (52-week high) to $2.66 (65% drawdown). Uninvestable without successful recapitalization. Even with capital raise, dilution likely 30–50%, eroding equity value. No near-term revenue path = structural downside. Survival binary; recovery low-probability.

rows.label: Lilium — Probability of Outperforming Sector Base Rate (12-month)

extras.prob_outperform_rationale: In asset liquidation / restructuring as of May 2026. Market cap collapsed to $32.89M from $3.2B peak (2021 SPAC merger) — 99% value destruction. Lilium Aerospace consortium seeking €200M recapitalization with low probability of success. Only outperforms (4% probability) if consortium rescue succeeds at terms favorable to legacy shareholders—tail-risk scenario. Historical case study of execution failure in eVTOL sector.
extras.funding_need_rationale: In constant restructuring/fundraising mode. Asset liquidation underway; every month requires capital to maintain operations. Probability: ~96%.
extras.driver_1_rationale: No operational activity; liquidation scenario. No revenue event within 12 months.
extras.driver_2_rationale: FAA certification path dormant. No near-term certification expected.
extras.driver_3_rationale: Liquidation restructuring; all new capital massively dilutive to legacy shareholders. Recovery value to common equity approaching zero.
extras.key_inflection: Consortium recapitalization outcome (€200M target, low probability). Lilium illustrates execution risk in eVTOL sector: delayed timelines, cash burn, inability to scale manufacturing, regulatory delays compounded. Archer's 252-aircraft target carries similar execution complexity; Lilium's failure is cautionary tale but not deterministic of Archer outcome (different capital base, partnerships, market timing).
